title: feat: track XCP-ng version with updatecli and parameterize download URLs
body:
## Summary

The XCP-ng version (`8.3.0-20250606`) is currently hardcoded in multiple places across the scripts. When a new XCP-ng release comes out, every reference must be updated manually — error-prone and easy to miss.

This issue tracks two changes:

### 1. Parameterize the XCP-ng version in scripts

Extract version-specific values into variables at the top of `build-iso.sh` (or a shared config file) so there is a **single source of truth**:

**Current state** (`build-iso.sh`):
```bash
NETINSTALL_URL="https://mirrors.xcp-ng.org/isos/8.3/xcp-ng-8.3.0-20250606-netinstall.iso"
NETINSTALL_SHA256_URL="https://mirrors.xcp-ng.org/isos/8.3/SHA256SUMS"
FULL_ISO_URL="https://mirrors.xcp-ng.org/isos/8.3/xcp-ng-8.3.0-20250606.iso"
OUTPUT_ISO="${WORK}/xcp-ng-8.3-unattended.iso"
```

**Target state**:
```bash
XCP_NG_MAJOR="8.3"
XCP_NG_VERSION="8.3.0-20250606"

NETINSTALL_URL="https://mirrors.xcp-ng.org/isos/${XCP_NG_MAJOR}/xcp-ng-${XCP_NG_VERSION}-netinstall.iso"
NETINSTALL_SHA256_URL="https://mirrors.xcp-ng.org/isos/${XCP_NG_MAJOR}/SHA256SUMS"
FULL_ISO_URL="https://mirrors.xcp-ng.org/isos/${XCP_NG_MAJOR}/xcp-ng-${XCP_NG_VERSION}.iso"
OUTPUT_ISO="${WORK}/xcp-ng-${XCP_NG_MAJOR}-unattended.iso"
```

Other files that reference the version and may need updating:
- `install-via-qemu.sh` (line 299: `xcp-ng-8.3-unattended.iso`)
- `build-iso.sh` (lines 253, 263: volume label `XCP-ng 8.3 Unattended`)

### 2. Add updatecli manifest to track new XCP-ng releases

Add an [updatecli](https://www.updatecli.io/) manifest that:
- Monitors the XCP-ng mirror (`https://mirrors.xcp-ng.org/isos/`) for new releases
- Automatically opens a PR when a new version is detected
- Updates the version variables introduced in step 1

**Possible approach**: use updatecli's `shell` or `htmlquery` source to scrape the mirror directory listing for the latest ISO filename, then update the version variables in the script.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] XCP-ng version is defined in a single place (variables at top of `build-iso.sh` or a shared config)
- [ ] All hardcoded version strings in scripts derive from those variables
- [ ] An updatecli manifest exists that can detect new XCP-ng releases
- [ ] updatecli can open a PR to bump the version automatically
- [ ] README documents the version update process
